summary: CortexFlow MVP tested end-to-end. Backend: 20/20 pytest pass (auth, projects, nodes, edges, GitHub repo connect+scan, Claude AI expand/generate-prompt, export markdown/json/agent_pack, node-types, auth enforcement). Frontend: Landing, register, login, dashboard, project creation, canvas load, command palette btn, sidebar tabs (Nodes/Repo), repo connect modal, logout all render. CRITICAL FRONTEND BUG: Inspector crashes with 'Cannot read properties of undefined (reading title)' the moment any node is added/selected on canvas — makes the inspector flow completely unusable.

ui_bugs.issue: TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'title') at Inspector. Root cause: useState(node) initializes 'draft' to undefined on first render (because node prop is initially undefined). useEffect that calls setDraft(node) runs AFTER render, but the render body accesses draft.title / draft.content / draft.file_references unconditionally once node is truthy. Result: as soon as a node is created (e.g. via sidebar double-click or command palette which auto-selects via setSelectedId(data.id)), the Inspector mounts with node defined but draft still undefined and React throws. FIX: use `const current = draft || node;` and read from `current.title` etc., OR initialize via lazy initializer + ensure draft is always synced before render (e.g. `if (!draft || draft.id !== node.id) { setDraft(node); return null }` early-return pattern, or guard each access `draft?.title || node.title || ''`).

Selector [data-testid="export-markdown-button"] not found. Either testid name differs (e.g. export-format-markdown / generate-export-button) or button is rendered only after format selection. Main agent should standardize testids: export-markdown-button, export-json-button, export-agent-pack-button, export-output.

integration_issues.issue: Both call addNodeAt which calls setSelectedId(data.id) immediately. This triggers the Inspector crash above. As a result, NO end-to-end node creation, editing, AI expand, or edge connection flow can be exercised via the UI right now.

Generate-prompt button clicked with no nodes (couldn't create nodes due to Inspector crash). prompt-output remained empty. Likely UI handles empty-context fine but needs re-test once Inspector bug is fixed.

action_items: FIX Inspector.jsx state init: ensure draft is never undefined when node is defined. Recommended: replace `const [draft, setDraft] = useState(node);` + useEffect pattern with derived value or guard: `const current = draft && draft.id === node.id ? draft : node;` and read fields from `current`. This is the #1 blocker.
Add data-testids for export buttons: export-markdown-button, export-json-button, export-agent-pack-button, export-output, and verify prompt-output testid is present on initial render.
After Inspector fix, re-test: sidebar dblclick creates node → inspector shows fields → autosave (350ms) persists → AI expand → edge connect → prompt engine output → export.
critical_code_review_comments: Inspector.jsx line 25-33: useState(node) + useEffect sync is a classic stale-state pitfall. Render uses draft.x without falling back to node; first render after a node is selected crashes.
CanvasPage.jsx addNodeAt auto-selects the new node, which is correct UX but exposes the Inspector bug.
server.py: JWT decode catches PyJWTError but raises bare HTTPException without `from None` — minor; works.
server.py @app.on_event('shutdown') is deprecated in FastAPI 0.110+ (use lifespan). Non-blocking.
Backend repo connect stores PAT in plaintext on user doc — explicitly called out as MVP-only in code comment; acceptable for MVP but flag for prod hardening.

success_rate.frontend: ~40% (auth+dashboard+navigation+landing+repo-modal-open OK; canvas interactivity blocked by Inspector crash)

test_credentials: Created on-the-fly fresh users with @cortexflow.dev domain (pydantic EmailStr accepts). Example: e2e_<timestamp>@cortexflow.dev / cortexflow123.
seed_data_creation: None persisted — all TEST_ prefixed projects cleaned up in fixtures.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend is solid — all 20 pytest tests pass (incl. real Claude calls and real GitHub octocat/Hello-World using branch 'master' not 'main'). Focus next iteration on frontend Inspector fix verification and the full canvas interaction loop (drag → select → edit → autosave → AI expand → edges → prompt engine output → export). Export testids may need renaming on the Console component. To skip the Inspector crash temporarily during testing, do NOT auto-select a node — but the bug must be fixed for the product to be usable.
rca of the issue: Inspector.jsx initializes draft via useState(node) where `node` is undefined on first mount. When CanvasPage later passes a real node prop, React re-renders Inspector BEFORE the useEffect calling setDraft(node) runs. The render body accesses draft.title (line ~114), draft.content (~149), and draft.file_references (~62) directly on an undefined draft — TypeError. Reproduction: register → create project → in canvas double-click any sidebar-node-* or press command palette button → Inspector mounts with selectedId set → crash overlay appears. Mitigation: derive a safe `current = (draft && draft.id === node.id) ? draft : node` and read all fields off `current`, or guard accesses with optional chaining + fallback to node.
